lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਨ ਮੋਹ ਦੋਨੋ ਕਉ ਪਰਹਰਿ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਕੇ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: maan moh dono kau parahar gobind ke gun gaavai |
lines.english: One who lays aside both pride and attachment, sings the Glorious Praises of the Lord of the Universe.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਗ ਜਗ ਨਿਹਫਲ ਤਿਹ ਮਾਨਉ ਜੋ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਜਸੁ ਬਿਸਰਾਵੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jog jag nihafal tih maanau jo prabh jas bisaraavai |1|
lines.english: Know that Yoga and sacrificial feasts are fruitless, if one forgets the Praises of God. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਇਹ ਬਿਧਿ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਾਨੀ ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਕਹਾਵੈ ॥੨॥੨॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak ih bidh ko praanee jeevan mukat kahaavai |2|2|
lines.english: Says Nanak, the mortal who does this is said to be 'jivan mukta' - liberated while yet alive. ||2||2||
Bilaaval, Ninth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਮੈ ਭਜਨੁ ਰਾਮ ਕੋ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: jaa mai bhajan raam ko naahee |
lines.english: There is no meditation on the Lord within him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰਥ ਕਰੈ ਬ੍ਰਤ ਫੁਨਿ ਰਾਖੈ ਨਹ ਮਨੂਆ ਬਸਿ ਜਾ ਕੋ ॥
lines.translit: teerath karai brat fun raakhai neh manooaa bas jaa ko |
lines.english: He bathes at sacred shrines of pilgrimage, and adheres to fasts, but he has no control over his mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਹ ਨਰ ਜਨਮੁ ਅਕਾਰਥੁ ਖੋਇਆ ਯਹ ਰਾਖਹੁ ਮਨ ਮਾਹੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tih nar janam akaarath khoeaa yeh raakhahu man maahee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: That man wastes his life uselessly - keep this in mind. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਹਫਲ ਧਰਮੁ ਤਾਹਿ ਤੁਮ ਮਾਨਹੁ ਸਾਚੁ ਕਹਤ ਮੈ ਯਾ ਕਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: nihafal dharam taeh tum maanahu saach kehat mai yaa kau |1|
lines.english: Know that such religion is useless to him. I speak the Truth for his sake. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਤੁਮ ਤਾਹਿ ਪਛਾਨਹੁ ਭਗਤਿ ਹੀਨ ਜੋ ਪ੍ਰਾਨੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: taise hee tum taeh pachhaanahu bhagat heen jo praanee |2|
lines.english: So, understand it: that mortal being who lacks devotional worship is just like that. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਾਹਨੁ ਜਲ ਮਹਿ ਰਾਖਿਓ ਭੇਦੈ ਨਾਹਿ ਤਿਹ ਪਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: jaise paahan jal meh raakhio bhedai naeh tih paanee |
lines.english: It's like a stone, kept immersed in water; still, the water does not penetrate it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਲ ਮੈ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨਾਮ ਤੇ ਪਾਵਤ ਗੁਰੁ ਯਹ ਭੇਦੁ ਬਤਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: kal mai mukat naam te paavat gur yeh bhed bataavai |
lines.english: In this Dark Age of Kali Yuga, liberation comes from the Naam. The Guru has revealed this secret.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਸੋਈ ਨਰੁ ਗਰੂਆ ਜੋ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੇ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਵੈ ॥੩॥੩॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak soee nar garooaa jo prabh ke gun gaavai |3|3|
lines.english: Says Nanak, he alone is a great man, who sings the Praises of God. ||3||3||
Bilaaval, Ashtpadheeyaa, First Mehl, Tenth House:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਭੈ ਪਇਐ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: vin bhai peiai bhagat na hoee |
lines.english: Without the Fear of God, there is no devotional worship.
Imbued with the Word of the Shabad, eternal peace is attained. ||1||
He dwells close at hand, and sees all,
but how rare is the Gurmukh who understands this.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਾਵਸਿ ਰਸਿ ਰਸਿ ਮਾਨੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh paavas ras ras maan |1| rahaau |
lines.english: obtaining it, the Gurmukhs enjoy the subtle essence of this nectar. ||1||Pause||
Such is the spiritual wisdom, the treasure of the Naam;

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਰਸ ਰਾਤੇ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: bin ras raate mukat na hoee |2|
lines.english: Without being imbued with the subtle essence, there is no liberation. ||2||
Everyone talks about spiritual wisdom and spiritual knowledge.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਥਿ ਕਥਿ ਬਾਦੁ ਕਰੇ ਦੁਖੁ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: kath kath baad kare dukh hoee |
lines.english: Talking, talking, they argue, and suffer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਥਿ ਕਹਣੈ ਤੇ ਰਹੈ ਨ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: kath kahanai te rahai na koee |
lines.english: No one can stop talking and discussing it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਕਥਨੀ ਹੈ ਪਰੁ ਰਹਤ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh kathanee hai par rehat na hoee |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh talks about it, but does not practice it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਆਨੁ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਸਭੁ ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: giaan dhiaan sabh gur te hoee |
lines.english: Spiritual wisdom and meditation all come from the Guru.
Through the lifestyle of Truth, the True Lord comes to dwell in the mind.
Maya has caught the mind in the trap of the whirlpool.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਬਿਆਪਿ ਰਹਿਓ ਬਿਖੁ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: ghatt ghatt biaap rahio bikh naal |
lines.english: Each and every heart is trapped by this bait of poison and sin.
Forgetting the Name, he finds no place of rest. ||3||
Your affairs shall be adjusted, if you contemplate the Lord in your heart. ||4||
See that whoever has come, is subject to death.
The self-willed, egotistical manmukh loses his honor.
The Creator Lord Himself inspires us to His devotional worship.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਗਿਆਨੀ ਜਿਨਿ ਸਬਦਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: so giaanee jin sabad liv laaee |
lines.english: He alone is a spiritual teacher, who lovingly focuses his consciousness on the Word of the Shabad.
He Himself blesses the Gurmukh with glorious greatness. ||5||
Those who lack the Naam, the Name of the Lord, are false, filthy and untouchable.
The life-night is dark, while the Divine Light is immaculate.
The Shaastras and Simritees implant the Naam within.
Listening, hearing and believing, one beholds the Divine Light. ||6||
The Vedas preach sermons of devotional worship.
The Gurmukh lives in peace and tranquility, doing deeds of sublime purity.
The self-willed manmukh suffers the pains of reincarnation.
His bonds are broken, enshrining the Name of the One Lord. ||7||
Says Nanak, there is no other at all. ||8||1||
Who should I see? There is none other than the Lord.
Believing in the Naam, one obtains true honor and adoration.
I see, and I say, that He alone is pleasing to my mind.
